curriculumRef.value: NCE Chemistry Year 1 — CHE 111 General Chemistry

blocks.title: Atomic number

citations.excerpt: The Atomic Number is the number of protons in the nucleus of an atom. It is the fundamental property that defines an element.

blocks.body: The **atomic number** is the number of protons in the nucleus. It defines the element.

citations.excerpt: In a neutral atom, the atomic number also equals the number of electrons.

questions.prompt: The atomic number counts which particle?
questions.feedback: The atomic number is the proton count; in a neutral atom it also equals the electron count.
